metaTitle: Squirrel Removal NYC | Wildlife Exclusion | The Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Humane squirrel removal and exclusion in NYC. Attic entry sealing and wildlife proofing. Licensed. Free inspection. Call or text now.
h1: Squirrel Removal in NYC — Out of Your Attic, Sealed Out for Good
heroParagraph: Squirrels in New York City attics are one of the most common wildlife calls we receive — and one of the most straightforward situations to resolve when handled correctly. Eastern gray squirrels are the dominant species in NYC and are year-round residents. They're active primarily during daylight hours, which makes them easier to identify (you'll hear movement in the morning and again in late afternoon, distinctly different from the nighttime activity of rats or raccoons). Squirrels enter through open fascia board gaps, deteriorated soffit material, and gaps at roof-to-wall junctions.
bodyParagraphs: The most common mistake in squirrel removal is sealing the entry point without confirming all animals have left. A squirrel sealed inside an attic will die, causing severe odor, attracting flies and secondary pests, and potentially leaving young that can't survive. The correct approach is exclusion — one-way doors or funnels are installed at the primary entry point so squirrels can exit but not re-enter, confirmed over 3–5 days, then the opening is sealed permanently. This approach is humane, effective, and avoids the sealed-animal problem entirely.

Squirrels also cause meaningful damage. They chew through electrical wiring (a fire hazard), tear insulation for nesting material, and, like raccoons, use a concentrated area of the attic as a latrine. NYC squirrels are extremely habituated to human presence and will attempt to re-enter the same structure at multiple points — sealing the primary entry without a thorough inspection for secondary gaps results in re-entry within days.
signs: Scratching, rolling, or scampering sounds in attic during morning and late afternoon (daytime activity distinguishes squirrels from rats)
Chewed or displaced soffit material, open gaps at fascia board or roof edge
Squirrel sightings on the roof, entering or exiting from the roofline area
Chewed wiring or electrical damage in attic — squirrels gnaw insulation off wiring
Insulation disturbed, compressed, or pulled into a concentrated nesting area
Acorns or food caches stored in corners of the attic
treatmentApproach: Squirrel exclusion uses one-way door devices installed at the primary entry point, confirmed active over 3–5 days, then removed and the opening permanently sealed with galvanized steel hardware cloth. We inspect the full roofline for secondary gaps — any opening larger than 1 inch (squirrels can compress their bodies significantly) is sealed proactively. Cage trapping is available when exclusion isn't practical for a specific entry point location. Attic inspection documents any wiring damage or insulation damage for insurance purposes. We provide a 90-day guarantee: if squirrels re-enter through a sealed point, we return at no charge.

faqs.q: How do squirrels get into the attic?
faqs.a: The most common entry points are deteriorated soffit material (especially at corners and junctions), open gaps where roof meets wall, and damaged fascia boards. Squirrels also enter through gable vents with damaged or missing screening. A gap as small as 1 inch is sufficient.

faqs.q: Will squirrels leave on their own?
faqs.a: Sometimes — female squirrels with young will move the young to a new location when the young are mobile. But they often return to the same site the following season, and the entry point they've established invites other squirrels to use it as well. Exclusion is the only permanent solution.

faqs.q: Can I just seal the hole myself?
faqs.a: You can, but only if you're 100% certain all animals have left. Check for activity over 2–3 days before sealing. If any animals are sealed inside — especially young — you'll have a dead animal odor problem within 1–2 weeks. One-way exclusion doors are safer because they guarantee all animals have exited before the hole is permanently sealed.

faqs.q: How much does squirrel removal cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Squirrel exclusion starts at $900 and ranges to $2,500 depending on entry point count, accessibility, and whether any secondary entry points are identified. Written estimate provided after inspection.

faqs.q: Can squirrels cause fires in my NYC home or building?
faqs.a: Yes — and this is one of the most underappreciated dangers of a squirrel infestation. Squirrels instinctively chew on hard materials to keep their incisors filed down, and electrical wiring is a frequent target. Chewed wiring is a leading cause of house fires attributed to rodents. If you hear squirrels in your walls or attic, an electrical inspection after removal is strongly advised.

faqs.q: How do I know if squirrels have already caused structural damage?
faqs.a: Signs include soft spots or discoloration on ceilings (from urine saturation), damaged insulation that's been displaced or shredded for nesting, chewed wood beams or joists visible in the attic, and compromised roof edges or fascia boards. Our technicians assess structural damage during the initial inspection and will advise you on what repairs are needed to prevent re-entry.
